executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware crisis with 104 organizations publicly listed as victims in a single 48-hour period. The most notable victim is Canada Goose, the iconic outerwear brand, claimed by the Coinbasecartel ransomware group. Qilin ransomware leads the assault with 23 victims, followed by Play (12) and Safepay (11). Critical sectors affected include education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart College), healthcare (multiple medical facilities), public sector (Peterborough Public Health, Municipality of North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé), and financial services (National Money Mart with confirmed database leak, Morison Insurance). No traditional CVE-based vulnerabilities were reported—this is purely an operational ransomware campaign wave.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 23 Canadian victims including Eastern Townships School Board, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), Waterloo Information Systems, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Pre-Con Builders, Questica, Watermark Beach Resort, CST Coal, Berts Electric, Maheu&Maheu, Capp Shupak, MG CPA, Bergmanis Preyra law firm, A Lococo Wholesale
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1048 (Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), double extortion model

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a ransomware emergency with 104 organizations breached in 48 hours across all provinces and sectors. High-profile victims include Canada Goose, National Money Mart (confirmed database leak), Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, Municipality of North Perth, and MRC de Maskinongé. No CCCS advisories were issued during this period despite the unprecedented volume of attacks. The dominant threat actors are Qilin (23 victims), Play (12), Safepay (11), and Clop (11), with education, healthcare, and public sector entities particularly impacted.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (luxury retail) claimed by Coinbasecartel; Active Green + Ross (automotive retail) claimed by Sinobi
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company - database leaked (Everest ransomware); Morison Insurance Brokers (Sinobi); Trucash (Lynx); multiple accounting/CPA firms including Pewarchuk CPA (Play), MG CPA (Qilin), reidhurstnagy.com (Incransom); Axion50plus financial services (Devman)

report_2w.executive_summary: A critical Linux kernel vulnerability (CVE-2026-31431, dubbed 'Copy Fail') is being actively exploited in the wild and was discovered with the assistance of AI-based research. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security has issued an alert for this vulnerability along with two other advisories covering a critical cPanel flaw (CVE-2026-41940) actively weaponized against government and MSP networks, and a warning about social engineering attacks targeting enterprise SaaS environments. Microsoft has documented a massive phishing campaign that compromised 35,000 users across 13,000 organizations in just three days using fake code of conduct notices. Multiple data breaches were disclosed including Vimeo (119,000 people), Trellix (source code), and DigiCert (via malicious screensaver), while ransomware groups ShinyHunters and Qilin remain highly active.

items.description: Linux kernel 'Copy Fail' local privilege escalation vulnerability allowing attackers to gain elevated access
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Low

items.description: Critical vulnerability in cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM) being weaponized to target government and MSP networks
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: High severity vulnerability in Oracle MCP Server Helper Tool (Oracle Open Source Projects)

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Vimeo (119,000 people), Instructure/Canvas (multiple educational institutions), ADT (5.5 million customers)
items.ttps: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage Object), Data Exfiltration, Extortion

items.name: Qilin (ransomware group)
items.targets: Star Precision, Ahorramas, various European organizations via UNC2465 affiliate
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), Ransomware deployment

items.name: Silver Fox (APT)
items.targets: Unknown organizations via tax-themed phishing
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Spearphishing Attachment), ValleyRAT deployment, ABCDoor backdoor deployment

items.targets: Various (historical)

items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1219 (Remote Access Software - SimpleHelp, ScreenConnect), Persistence via legitimate RMM

items.name: UNC2465 (Qilin affiliate)
items.targets: European organizations distributing Qilin ransomware
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), Ransomware affiliate operations

items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1071.001 (Application Layer Protocol - Telegram), AppleJeus malware, Espionage

items.ttps: T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise), T1199 (Trusted Relationship), GitHub Actions abuse

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued three critical advisories this week: AL26-009 for the actively exploited Linux CVE-2026-31431, AL26-008 for the critical cPanel vulnerability CVE-2026-41940 being weaponized against government and MSP networks (explicitly mentioning Canada), and AL26-010 warning of social engineering attacks targeting enterprise SaaS environments. A data breach investigation has ensnared an Alberta separatist movement related to an independence petition, though no major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were reported compromised this period.
